articles.headline: Pakistan targets Afghanistan's Bagram airbase in major retaliatory strike
articles.summary: Pakistan conducted over 50 airstrikes on military targets in Afghanistan, including the destruction of a hangar at Bagram airbase. The strikes are a response to alleged Taliban-backed aggression across the border.

articles.headline: Over 18.9 million eligible voters to cast ballots in Nepal today
articles.summary: Nepal holds crucial general elections on Thursday following the collapse of the previous government due to Gen Z-led protests. Voting across the country will conclude at 5 pm with immediate counting planned thereafter.

articles.headline: MEA sets up control room for Indians in war zone
articles.summary: A 24-hour control room has been established in New Delhi to assist the nearly one crore Indian citizens in West Asia. The Indian embassy has already moved students from Tehran to safer regional locations.

articles.headline: Umrah pilgrimage from Kerala severely disrupted by flight cancellations
articles.summary: Scores of pilgrims in Kerala are in limbo after nearly 60% of flights to Mecca were cancelled due to hostilities. Travel agents warn that postponing trips is difficult as the Haj season is approaching.

articles.headline: Explosions reported in Tehran and Jerusalem as conflict escalates
articles.summary: The US-Israel war with Iran entered its fifth day with intense bombing and missile interceptions across both capitals. Over 1,000 deaths have been reported in Iran since the conflict began on Saturday.

articles.headline: US defense secretary warns of potential Iranian missile impacts
articles.summary: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth acknowledged that some Iranian air attacks may still hit their targets despite American air superiority. He stated that the US has deployed maximum resources to protect forces as regional risks remain high.

articles.headline: Saudi Aramco refinery targeted by attempted drone attack
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia’s Ras Tanura refinery, which houses its largest domestic facility, was targeted by a drone attack on Wednesday. Authorities confirmed there was no damage or disruption to oil supplies following the incident.

articles.headline: Nepal conducts general elections following historic anti corruption protests
articles.summary: Over 18 million eligible voters in Nepal will elect members to the House of Representatives on Thursday. The election is the first since GenZ-led protests led to the ouster of the previous K P Sharma Oli government.

articles.headline: US private payrolls grow by sixty three thousand jobs in February
articles.summary: ADP's national employment report showed the largest increase in US private payrolls in seven months. However, data for January was revised downward, and gains remained concentrated in health and education services.

articles.headline: US military kills Iranian official allegedly behind Trump assassination plot
articles.summary: The Pentagon announced the killing of an unnamed Iranian official who reportedly led a unit tasked with assassinating Donald Trump. The strike took place during the ongoing conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran.

articles.headline: Italy considers sending air defense systems to Gulf nations amid strikes
articles.summary: The Italian government is likely to provide military aid, including SAMP/T batteries, to Gulf states facing Iranian air attacks. Anti-drone systems are also being assessed for rapid transfer to requested nations.

articles.headline: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz causing global energy prices to spike
articles.summary: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has blocked the maritime choke point that carries one-fifth of global oil and gas shipments. Brent crude prices have surged past $80 per barrel following attacks on passing vessels.

articles.headline: Indian expats in Dubai worried as regional conflict threatens safety
articles.summary: Recent drone attacks near residential areas have impacted the Gulf's image as a stable haven for investors. Over 9 million Indians work in West Asia, contributing significantly to national remittances.

articles.headline: Toyoda family cements control over Toyota Industries after Elliott battle
articles.summary: A ₹6.7 trillion buyout deal will privatize the group's founding loom-making affiliate. The move follows a protracted battle with activist fund Elliott Investment Management over corporate governance.

articles.headline: US military success against Iran could stabilize global oil security
articles.summary: Analysts suggest that neutralizing threats to the Strait of Hormuz would reduce the 'geopolitical premium' in oil prices. A shift in regimes could eventually return millions of barrels to the market.

articles.headline: US and Chinese trade negotiators to meet ahead of leaders' summit
articles.summary: Trade chiefs will convene in Paris to discuss business deals involving Boeing aircraft and agricultural exports. The talks precede a planned meeting between Donald Trump and Xi Jinping in China.

articles.headline: Pakistan forces kill 67 Afghan Taliban personnel in overnight operations
articles.summary: Security forces repulsed attacks at 16 locations along the south-western border. The ongoing Operation Ghazab was launched in response to raids that began in late February.

articles.headline: US Navy reports dramatic increase in Chinese nuclear submarine production
articles.summary: China is projected to deploy advanced Type 096 submarines capable of hitting the US from protected waters by 2030. The new vessels will feature substantial advancements in noise quieting and reactor design.

articles.headline: Chinese Banks Reshape Global Loan Market with Cheap Credit
articles.summary: Flush with low-cost funds, Chinese lenders are undercutting international rivals by extending cheaper credit across Asia. This expansion comes as domestic demand in China weakens, forcing banks to seek overseas infrastructure and commodity projects.

articles.headline: Singapore and Taiwan Identify $700 Million Tied to Alleged Scam
articles.summary: Authorities have uncovered illicit funds linked to the Prince Holding Group and its founder Chen Zhi. Total identified assets in the transnational case have now surpassed $1 billion across Asian jurisdictions.

articles.headline: Hormuz Strait Shipping Crisis Chokes Global Oil and Gas Flows
articles.summary: At least 200 ships are waiting in open waters as the US-Iran war paralyzes the world's most vital energy waterway. Qatar has suspended gas output and Iraq has cut oil production due to a lack of export routes.

articles.headline: Mojtaba Khamenei Emerges as Favoured Successor to Slain Iranian Leader
articles.summary: The powerful son of Ali Khamenei is reportedly alive and leading the race to become the next Supreme Leader. The selection comes as Israel vows to target any new leadership appointed by the Iranian regime.

articles.headline: Trump Government Proposes Performance-based Layoffs for Federal Workers
articles.summary: A new 102-page proposal from the White House aims to substitute job performance for seniority in determining federal layoffs. The plan would also make it easier for agencies to dismiss new hires and probationary employees.

articles.headline: Palestinian envoy slams military force as ‘jungle law’
articles.summary: Ambassador Abdullah M. Abu Shawesh criticized major powers for abandoning negotiations in favor of military muscle in the Iran-U.S. conflict. He warned that the escalation is stripping defenceless nations of their future.

articles.headline: U.S. and Israel continue heavy air strikes across Iran
articles.summary: Multiple Iranian cities, including Tehran and Qom, were hit in air strikes on the fifth day of the war. Israel reported shooting down an Iranian fighter jet, while Iran responded with drone attacks on U.S. bases in Qatar.

articles.headline: Iran seeks swift appointment of new Supreme Leader
articles.summary: Following the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in air strikes, the Assembly of Experts is working to select a successor. Iranian authorities have postponed public mourning ceremonies citing the intensity of ongoing military strikes.

articles.headline: EU ready to defend trade interests against Trump threats
articles.summary: The European Commission stated it will safeguard member interests after U.S. President Donald Trump threatened to sever trade with Spain. The threat follows Spain's refusal to allow its bases to be used for attacks on Iran.

articles.headline: Sensex tanks 1,123 points as West Asia conflict triggers market sell-off
articles.summary: Escalating geopolitical tensions led to a massive stock market crash and pushed the rupee to a record low of 92.15 against the dollar. Foreign investors aggressively sold domestic stocks as crude oil prices surged.

articles.headline: Karnataka's debt set to increase as revenue strain dogs finances
articles.summary: The state's total liabilities are projected to cross Rs 7.6 lakh crore in the next fiscal year. While within fiscal limits, a large portion of borrowing is likely to fund revenue expenditure rather than assets.

articles.headline: Gold surges as safe-haven demand increases due to multi-pronged attacks
articles.summary: Spot gold rose 1.2% to over $5,146 per ounce as investors sought safety amid global instability. Analysts predict the yellow metal could reach $6,000 if geopolitical tensions in the Gulf continue to escalate.

articles.headline: US Treasury Secretary flags potential tariff hike to 15 percent
articles.summary: Scott Bessent indicated that universal levies could be increased soon to replace previous trade regimes invalidated by the Supreme Court. He also played down oil market risks, citing ample global supply outside the Gulf.

articles.headline: Mumbai fuel stations see surge in demand amid Gulf tensions
articles.summary: Motorists rushed to fill tanks on Wednesday, leading to a 30-35% spike in demand at petrol pumps. Fear of supply disruptions and price hikes due to the Iran-US conflict fueled the panic.

articles.headline: Rupee hits record 92.3 low; Sensex crashes 1.4% on war
articles.summary: Indian markets tumbled as the Middle East conflict dented investor sentiment and spiked oil prices. The Sensex dropped 1,122 points while the rupee became Asia's worst-performing currency this year.

articles.headline: EU unveils local content requirements for green manufacturing sectors
articles.summary: The European Commission introduced the Industrial Accelerator Act (IAA) to boost the competitiveness of its manufacturing sector. The plan sets local content and low-carbon requirements for public procurement of steel, cement, and electric vehicles.

articles.headline: RBI project finance guidelines pressure real estate lenders
articles.summary: New RBI rules for project finance are causing concerns for lenders regarding mandated debt-to-equity ratios. Executives noted that requiring higher upfront loan amounts increases risk and could lead to developer downgrades.

articles.headline: Global oil prices rise amid potential Strait of Hormuz disruptions
articles.summary: Brent crude prices face upward pressure as 40% of India's oil imports transit through the critical Strait of Hormuz. A full closure of the shipping route could push prices into the $100-$110 per barrel range, impacting global supply chains.

articles.headline: US Treasury Secretary says Trump tariff hike likely this week
articles.summary: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced that a global import tariff increase to 15 per cent from 10 per cent is imminent. This follows the Supreme Court striking down previous tariffs under a national emergencies law.

articles.headline: Sebi considers revamping position limits for commodity derivatives market
articles.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India is reviewing market-wide position limits and margin frameworks to boost participation in commodity derivatives. Discussions also addressed the prolonged trading ban on seven key agri-commodities.

articles.headline: Net inflows into gold and silver ETFs moderated in February
articles.summary: Estimated net inflows into gold ETFs fell to approximately ₹7,000 crore in February after reaching a record ₹24,040 crore in January. The moderation follows a surge in gold prices and potential profit booking by investors.

articles.headline: Escalating West Asia conflict triggers sharp fall in Indian stock markets
articles.summary: The Sensex dropped 1.4 per cent to an 11-month low as oil prices spiked due to the Iran-Israel conflict. Investor wealth worth ₹21.5 trillion was erased over three sessions amid heavy selling by foreign portfolio investors.

articles.headline: Tighter RBI norms for capital market intermediaries to hit bank income
articles.summary: New Reserve Bank of India rules bar bank financing for proprietary trading by brokers and increase collateral requirements. Private sector banks with higher exposure to intermediaries are expected to see a decline in fee income.

articles.headline: Maharashtra approves private investment policy to revive struggling cooperative sugar mills
articles.summary: A new state policy allows financially stressed cooperative sugar factories to partner with private developers for byproduct processing projects. Projects like ethanol units and biogas plants will be developed under build-operate-transfer models.

articles.headline: Indian fertilizer production declines as West Asia conflict halts LNG supply
articles.summary: Urea manufacturers have started cutting output due to the suspension of Qatari LNG shipments, which serve as a critical feedstock. A prolonged disruption could lead to facility shutdowns and higher agricultural production costs.

articles.headline: India services sector growth slows slightly in February as demand moderates
articles.summary: The HSBC India Services PMI eased to 58.1 in February from 58.5 in January, reflecting a 13-month low in new order growth. Despite the slowdown, the sector remains in expansion territory supported by international sales and increased hiring.

articles.headline: Sidbi cancels eight thousand crore rupee bond issuance due to high yields
articles.summary: The Small Industries Development Bank of India scrapped its scheduled bond sale after bids came in at higher yields than expected. Market sentiment has dampened as global financial instability rises following geopolitical tensions in West Asia.

articles.headline: India faces storage strain as central grain stocks exceed buffer requirements
articles.summary: Central stocks of rice and wheat are currently 178 per cent above the required buffer norms, creating significant storage and fiscal challenges. Policy experts call for aligning procurement with distribution needs to reduce the ₹2 trillion subsidy bill.

articles.headline: New Delhi monitors energy stockpile as West Asia conflict threatens supplies
articles.summary: State-run oil marketing companies maintain 25 days of crude and fuel stocks amid disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. India imports 90% of its crude needs, with half currently transiting the impacted passageway.

articles.headline: India's current account deficit widens to 1.3% of GDP in Q3
articles.summary: Reserve Bank data shows the trade gap has widened amid net capital outflows of $3.7 billion. Geopolitical tensions in West Asia are expected to further pressure the Indian rupee and oil import bills.

articles.headline: India's revamped GDP series reveals smaller economy and weaker private consumption
articles.summary: The new 2022-23 base year series downwardly revised nominal GDP by up to 3.8% for recent years. Methodological changes and new survey data confirmed that previous proxy indicators led to overestimations.

articles.headline: Japan and India discuss joint rare earth mining in Rajasthan
articles.summary: Tokyo seeks to reduce reliance on China by helping India extract hard rock rare earth deposits. These minerals are essential for manufacturing electric vehicle motors and wind turbines.

articles.headline: ADB to provide $1.2 billion for India's clean energy transition
articles.summary: The Asian Development Bank will fund rooftop solar initiatives and power distribution reforms in 2026. The assistance includes support for the PM Surya Ghar scheme and green skilling for vulnerable groups.

articles.headline: India's white-collar job market grows 12% in February
articles.summary: The Naukri JobSpeak Index reached 3,233 points, driven by AI adoption and a recovery in the IT sector. Hiring for AI/ML roles surged by 40% compared to the previous year.

articles.headline: EPFO maintains PF interest rate at 8.25% despite RBI cuts
articles.summary: The Employees’ Provident Fund Organisation has decided to hold payouts steady based on investment earnings. This decision benefits over 70 million active subscribers who lack other social security nets.

articles.headline: The 'orange economy' offers India path to gender-inclusive growth
articles.summary: Creative and cultural industries now account for 3.1% of global GDP and employ a high percentage of women. India's strategic push in AVGC sectors aims to capture this 'youth dividend'.

articles.headline: Finance Commission proposes abolishing revenue deficit grants to states
articles.summary: The move aims to reduce moral hazard and encourage states to improve revenue mobilization. States like Punjab and Himachal Pradesh face significant adjustment burdens under the new framework.

articles.headline: Iran-Israel Tensions Pressure Engineering and Capital Goods Stocks
articles.summary: The BSE Capital Goods index has dropped nearly 4% over three sessions due to concerns over project cancellations in West Asia. Companies like L&T and KEC International are facing pressure due to their significant regional exposure.

articles.headline: Indian Equity Indices Tumble 1.5% Amid Escalating West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: The Nifty and Sensex saw their highest single-day falls since the budget as rising oil prices spooked investors. Foreign portfolio investors sold shares worth over 8,700 crore in a single day.

articles.headline: Basmati Rice Prices Fall Locally Amid West Asia Export Disruptions
articles.summary: Domestic basmati prices have dropped 5% to 6% as exports to the Middle East face significant hurdles. Nearly 200,000 tonnes of rice are currently stuck at ports due to the regional conflict.

articles.headline: Karnataka Disputes RBI Assessment of State Liability Projections
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has challenged the RBI's report that state liabilities would reach 26.5% of GSDP. The state argues the figures include Union-backed loans and earmarked funds that do not represent actual debt obligations.

articles.headline: Aluminium Prices Hit Four-Year High as Bahrain Smelter Declares Force Majeure
articles.summary: Global aluminium prices jumped 5% after Aluminium Bahrain warned of delivery delays due to the Strait of Hormuz closure. The region supplied 8% of the world's aluminium last year, causing supply fears for construction and transport.

articles.headline: India's Volatility Index Surges 50% as Geopolitical Conflict Deepens
articles.summary: The India VIX logged its steepest two-day advance since the 2020 Covid shock, reaching its highest level in 10 months. Analysts warn that market sentiment will remain cautious until stability or a ceasefire is reached in West Asia.

articles.headline: Wealth Advisors Urge Caution and Portfolio Protection Amid Global Heat
articles.summary: Financial planners are advising investors to avoid aggressive equity bets and focus on rebalancing portfolios with debt and gold. Geopolitical tensions have dragged the Nifty down 7% from its recent peak.

articles.headline: Bank Credit-Deposit Ratio Hits Record 82.5% as Liquidity Thins
articles.summary: Indian banks are lending a higher proportion of their deposits, leading to a record issuance of certificates of deposit. Lenders are turning to short-term market borrowings to bridge the gap between credit and deposit growth.

articles.headline: Services Sector PMI Eases to 58.1 in February but Remains Robust
articles.summary: India's services activity lost slight momentum as new business growth reached a 13-month low. However, business confidence climbed to a one-year high as firms look to broaden their market presence.

articles.headline: India Scraps Soya Oil Cargoes as Prices Rise Relative to Palm Oil
articles.summary: The world's top edible oil importer has cancelled nearly 33,000 tonnes of soybean oil orders due to high premiums. Traders are shifting to cheaper palm oil options as West Asia tensions disrupt energy and biodiesel supplies.

articles.headline: Indian stock indices tumble amid global oil market uncertainty
articles.summary: The Sensex and Nifty 50 dropped 1.4% and 1.5% respectively as oil prices surged following the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. Investors continued selling for a second consecutive session due to West Asian conflict escalation.

articles.headline: Indian bond yields rise following spike in crude oil
articles.summary: Yields on the benchmark 10-year government security hit 6.7112% as Brent crude neared $85 per barrel. Traders are selling off bonds due to fears of persistent inflation and a widening trade deficit from the West Asian conflict.

articles.headline: Government to probe alleged irregularities in KPSC recruitment exams
articles.summary: Home Minister G Parameshwara said the government will examine claims that candidates from the same room or family cleared the mains exam. The opposition BJP has called for results to be withheld pending a fair investigation.

articles.headline: Madiga community threatens protest over delayed internal reservation matrix
articles.summary: Left sect SC groups have called for a major protest starting March 12, opposing the government's recent move to fill 56,432 posts. The community warns of political fallout for Congress in upcoming assembly byelections.

articles.headline: Protests over Khamenei killing barred in Bengaluru city
articles.summary: The Home Minister announced that no rallies or processions to condemn the killing of Iran's leader will be allowed without prior permission. Future permitted protests must be restricted to Freedom Park after a police review.

articles.headline: BJP worker and two others stabbed in Udupi beach clash
articles.summary: A group armed with knives attacked a local BJP worker and his friends near a guest house on Malpe Beach. Police have identified the suspects and are investigating the dispute related to a family matter.

articles.headline: Jaishankar discusses West Asia conflict with EU’s Kaja Kallas
articles.summary: The External Affairs Minister held a telephonic conversation with the European Commission vice-president regarding de-escalation in the Gulf. India expressed strong interest in regional peace and the safety of its large expatriate community.

articles.headline: DMK and Congress end impasse with Tamil Nadu seat-sharing deal
articles.summary: The DMK has allotted 28 assembly seats and one Rajya Sabha berth to the Congress for the upcoming elections. Sonia Gandhi reportedly intervened to settle the deal and maintain the alliance against the BJP.

articles.headline: Election Commission cleans up Telangana voter rolls after report
articles.summary: Authorities corrected absurd polling station names like 'Cashew Lizard' and 'For Pakistan' that appeared in official lists. The CEO stated the errors were due to faulty Telugu-to-English software translations during data uploads.

articles.headline: Three people die by suicide in Bengal over voter list scrutiny
articles.summary: Families claim that victims in Jalpaiguri and Malda took extreme steps due to extreme anxiety over the SIR adjudication process. The state government has blamed the Election Commission for the growing panic among citizens.

articles.headline: Nitish Kumar may move to Rajya Sabha; son potentially deputy CM
articles.summary: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar is speculated to file nominations for the Upper House on Thursday. Sources indicate his son, Nishant Kumar, might be inducted as a Deputy CM following the transition.

articles.headline: Rohit Pawar alleges factual errors in Baramati plane crash report
articles.summary: The NCP (SP) MLA claimed the preliminary probe into the crash that killed Ajit Pawar contains discrepancies regarding tree collisions. He further alleged the aircraft operator had previously risked the CM's life.

articles.headline: Maharashtra suspends 316 officials over fraudulent disability certificates
articles.summary: Disciplinary action was taken against Zilla Parishad employees who used fake credentials to gain jobs under the Divyang quota. Verification across 28 districts revealed many had no valid documentation.

articles.headline: PM Modi welcomes Finnish President Stubb to strengthen bilateral ties
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi greeted President Alexander Stubb during his state visit to New Delhi. Discussions will focus on trade, regional issues, and Stubb's keynote at the Raisina Dialogue.

articles.headline: Srinagar MP Ruhullah Mehdi booked for misleading social media content
articles.summary: Jammu and Kashmir police registered FIRs against MP Ruhullah Mehdi and former mayor Junaid Mattu. They are accused of circulating fabricated information regarding the US-Israel attacks on Iran.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu CM Stalin slams Centre over Hindi imposition
articles.summary: MK Stalin condemned the naming of a railway entrance arch in Tiruchirappalli as 'Kartavya Dwar'. He warned that the BJP government is undermining the self-respect of Tamils by promoting Sanskrit names.

articles.headline: Ministry of External Affairs sets up control room for Indians in West Asia
articles.summary: The Indian government has established a 24-hour helpline to assist approximately 10 million citizens living in the Gulf region. The move comes as escalating conflict prompts safety concerns for Indian seafarers and students.

articles.headline: Kerala Governor signs bill making Malayalam the sole official language of administration
articles.summary: Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar has approved the Malayalam Language Bill 2025, mandating its use for state administration. The legislation includes safeguards for linguistic minorities following concerns raised by the Karnataka government.

articles.headline: Congress backs Sharad Pawar as candidate for Rajya Sabha elections in Maharashtra
articles.summary: The Congress has declared its support for NCP chief Sharad Pawar in the upcoming March 16 Rajya Sabha polls. The decision follows discussions within the Maha Vikas Aghadi alliance regarding the single winnable seat.

articles.headline: Prime Minister Modi calls for India to become global manufacturing partner
articles.summary: Modi urged businesses to leverage new FTAs and focus on high-quality standards to attract world markets. He highlighted recent MSME reforms aimed at removing growth fears among small enterprises.

articles.headline: Rohit Pawar Alleges Iran Threatened CM Shinde's Flight in 2023
articles.summary: NCP (SP) leader Rohit Pawar claimed that Iranian authorities threatened to shoot down a chartered aircraft carrying Eknath Shinde last year. He cited the incident as an example of safety negligence by the air charter company involved.

articles.headline: TVK Founder Vijay Calls Upcoming Tamil Nadu Polls an Oceanic Emotion
articles.summary: Actor-turned-politician Vijay urged voters to give his party a chance in the 2026 assembly elections. He criticized the ruling DMK as an 'evil force' and focused on agrarian issues in the Cauvery delta.

articles.headline: Nitish Kumar may resign as Bihar CM for Rajya Sabha seat
articles.summary: JD(U) leader Nitish Kumar is expected to file his nomination for the Rajya Sabha, potentially ending his 20-year tenure as Chief Minister. Sources indicate a BJP candidate from the backward classes will likely succeed him.

articles.headline: DMK and Congress seal seat-sharing pact for Tamil Nadu election
articles.summary: The Congress will contest 28 seats and receive one Rajya Sabha berth as part of an alliance agreement with the ruling DMK. The pact ends days of uncertainty and confirms the continuation of their 22-year partnership.

articles.headline: Tiruchi railway office removes Hindi words following political row
articles.summary: Authorities at the Tiruchi Railway Division removed the Hindi term 'Kartavya Dwar' from a new entrance arch after protests from DMK workers. Chief Minister M.K. Stalin described the use of Hindi names as a form of imposition.

articles.headline: Senior CPI(M) leader G. Sudhakaran signals break with party
articles.summary: Former Minister G. Sudhakaran did not apply for membership renewal, alleging he has been ridiculed and isolated by the Kerala State leadership. The move comes as the party prepares its candidate list for the 2026 Assembly election.

articles.headline: Karnataka recruitment drive triggers divide over internal reservation
articles.summary: The Congress government’s plan to fill 56,432 posts has been complicated by a decision to cap reservation at 50%. This has upset Dalit left communities who were promised proportional internal quotas in the 2023 manifesto.

articles.headline: MVA names Sharad Pawar as sole Rajya Sabha candidate
articles.summary: The Maha Vikas Aghadi formally announced Sharad Pawar's candidacy after the Congress dropped its own claim to the seat. The move increases the likelihood of an unopposed contest in Maharashtra.

articles.headline: Raijor Dal releases first list of 11 candidates for Assam
articles.summary: Led by Akhil Gogoi, the Raijor Dal announced its initial candidates less than 24 hours after ally Congress released its list. The move suggests ongoing disagreements over seat-sharing for the 126-member House.

articles.headline: Omar Abdullah chairs peace meeting with Kashmir civil society
articles.summary: The J&K Chief Minister coordinated with religious leaders and legislators to maintain harmony following developments in West Asia. Participants expressed condolences for lives lost in Iran and urged responsible public expression.

articles.headline: Over 1,500 flights cancelled in 4 days due to West Asia crisis
articles.summary: The civil aviation ministry reported massive flight disruptions as Indian and foreign carriers avoided high-risk airspace in the Gulf. Air India is increasing capacity on key long-haul routes to manage stranded passengers.

articles.headline: Bengaluru police to trial right-side driving at congested junctions
articles.summary: Authorities plan to implement American-style right-side driving on specific road stretches to reduce bottlenecks. The system has already shown success in a pilot run near Garuda Mall over several years.

articles.headline: BMTC launches 'Vajra Vistara' AC bus services to satellite towns
articles.summary: The Bangalore Metropolitan Transport Corporation has expanded its jurisdiction to 40km, launching premium AC services to Ramanagara and Kanakapura. The express trips aim to serve daily commuters using Namma Metro connections.

articles.headline: Shopkeepers on SJP Road protest months of infrastructure neglect
articles.summary: Traders in Bengaluru's 'Spare Parts Road' are struggling with broken footpaths and open drains left by pipeline works. Local businesses report dropped footfall and unsafe conditions for both pedestrians and vehicle movement.

articles.headline: Bengaluru proposes 101 skywalks and fixes for 137 waterlogging spots
articles.summary: Traffic authorities and the GBA have unveiled an infrastructure plan to improve pedestrian movement and monsoon resilience. The projects include relocating misplaced bus stops and using pipe-pushing technology for drain repairs.

articles.headline: Railways implement airport-style boarding at SMVT to handle Holi rush
articles.summary: South Western Railway introduced regulated entry and structured queues to manage thousands of passengers heading home for the festival. Additional holding zones were created at major city stations to prevent platform overcrowding.

articles.headline: Bengaluru North city corporation procures dry waste vacuum machine
articles.summary: The corporation has purchased a Rs 65-lakh truck-mounted machine to clear roadside litter and waste from vacant sites. The machine features a 5,000-litre capacity and can also be used for drain cleaning.

articles.headline: Airlines clear Gulf flyer backlog based on original booking dates
articles.summary: Passengers stranded in the West Asia region are being rebooked chronologically as flight operations slowly resume. Travelers have reported significant delays of over a week, causing them to miss various personal and work commitments.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu launches new shipbuilding policy to boost maritime sector
articles.summary: The policy offers incentives for shipyards, green recycling, and luxury yacht construction to make the state a global maritime hub. It focuses on sustainable technology and attracting investment through world-class infrastructure.

articles.headline: Maharashtra government claims Wilson Gym failed to seek lease renewal
articles.summary: Revenue Minister Bawankule stated that the city collector received no application for renewing the Wilson College Gymkhana lease. The land has been leased to the Jain International Organisation for 30 years.

articles.headline: Mumbai road concretisation drive causes widespread disruption and delays
articles.summary: Ongoing roadwork in Sion, Sandhurst Road, and Borivali has breached multiple deadlines, causing business losses and traffic chaos. Residents have complained about poor contractor oversight and lack of site safety.

articles.headline: High Court seeks MMRDA response on Mumbai metro safety audit
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court directed MMRDA to reply to a PIL seeking third-party safety audits of all metro sites. This follows a fatal slab collapse in Mulund that killed one person last month.

articles.headline: Shipping ministry tracks Indian vessels in Gulf 24/7 amid war
articles.summary: A monitoring centre was established for Indian-flagged ships near the Strait of Hormuz following the Iran-US conflict. The ministry is coordinating with the Navy to ensure safe passage for 27 commercial vessels.

articles.headline: States leverage power exchanges to optimize procurement and revenue
articles.summary: Utility companies in states like Uttarakhand and West Bengal are utilizing power exchanges to reduce costs and generate revenue. Uttarakhand Power Corporation reportedly saved over ₹160 crore by diversifying procurement away from higher-priced contracts.

articles.headline: Indian carriers resume West Asia services as flight restrictions begin to ease
articles.summary: Airlines including Air India and IndiGo are gradually restoring connectivity to West Asia following safety reviews of regional airspace. Additional capacity is being deployed to handle high demand for repatriation and regular travel.

articles.headline: India prepares storage plan for natural gas to secure supplies
articles.summary: The Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board is proposing overground LNG storage facilities near existing terminals. The move seeks to buffer India's 55% gas import dependency against regional supply blockades.

articles.headline: Power ministry extends comment period for Draft National Electricity Policy
articles.summary: The deadline for stakeholder feedback on the 2026 policy has been moved to March 19. The draft aims to address discom debt, grid resilience, and variable renewable energy integration.

articles.headline: Stalin Launches Development Drive and Signs 5,300-crore MoU with MRF
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu CM MK Stalin inaugurated infrastructure projects and signed a deal for a new tyre manufacturing plant in Sivaganga. He also released new state policies for shipbuilding and agro-forestry to boost the local economy.

articles.headline: Airlines Operate Special Repatriation Flights as West Asia Airspace Closes
articles.summary: IndiGo and Air India are deploying additional wide-body aircraft to rescue stranded passengers following the cancellation of over 500 flights. Hubs like Dubai and Qatar remain disrupted, forcing carriers to recalibrate international routes.

articles.headline: Over 180 flights cancelled at Indian metros due to war
articles.summary: Airspace restrictions in West Asia led to the cancellation of 93 flights in Mumbai, 52 in Delhi, and 34 in Bengaluru. Air India and IndiGo are deploying additional aircraft to manage stranded passengers on international routes.

articles.headline: India's breast cancer incidence more than doubles in 33 years
articles.summary: A study published in The Lancet Oncology reveals that breast cancer cases in India rose from 13 to 29.4 per lakh women since 1990. Experts attribute the rise to modifiable risk factors like diet and lifestyle.

articles.headline: Poor lifestyle habits leading to early tooth loss in young adults
articles.summary: Dentists in Bengaluru report a spike in patients in their 30s requiring implants due to tobacco use and high sugar intake. Professionals in the tech sector are particularly affected due to stress and delayed dental visits.

articles.headline: IVF centre director arrested in illegal human egg extraction racket
articles.summary: Badlapur police arrested Dr. Amol Patil, director of Malti IVF Centre, in connection with an unauthorized surrogacy and egg trafficking scam. This is the sixth arrest in a case involving poor women as donors.

articles.headline: Actor Rajshri Deshpande reveals breast cancer diagnosis and successful surgery
articles.summary: The Sacred Games star shared on Instagram that she was diagnosed with Grade 1 Infiltrating ductal carcinoma. She underwent surgery and thanked her family and fans for their support during the ordeal.

articles.headline: Serum Institute receives approval for combined Covid and influenza vaccine trials
articles.summary: India's drug regulator has cleared Phase III clinical trials for a single-dose trivalent nanoparticle vaccine. The combo shot aims to simplify immunization schedules and reduce vaccine fatigue.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh Plans PPP Model for 10 New Medical Colleges
articles.summary: The state government aims to operationalize 10 medical colleges under the Public-Private Partnership model over the next two years. The total cost for these projects is estimated at 8,480 crore.

articles.headline: FDA Says Novo Nordisk Ad for Ozempic is Misleading Viewers
articles.summary: The US FDA has issued a notice against Novo Nordisk for a TV advertisement that lacks supporting data for superiority claims. The agency stated the ad implies broader eligibility for the drug than is actually approved for heart and kidney conditions.

articles.headline: Petronet LNG and MRPL invoke force majeure amid Gulf shipping crisis
articles.summary: India's top gas importer and Mangalore Refinery have suspended exports and notified suppliers as vessels cannot reach loading ports. The disruption is linked to heightened maritime security risks in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: Indian tea exporters hold nerve as shipments to West Asia stall
articles.summary: Industry experts are monitoring the crisis as nearly 40% of India's tea exports head to markets like Iran and Iraq. Concerns remain regarding payment systems and maritime logistics through the UAE.

articles.headline: MSC to drop Gulf cargo at nearest safe ports during hostilities
articles.summary: The world's largest carrier announced it will offload containers at alternative seaports due to safety risks in the Gulf. Shippers will be responsible for alternate transportation and local port fees for affected shipments.

articles.headline: Saudi Aramco refinery targeted by drone attack; no damage reported
articles.summary: The Ras Tanura domestic refinery and export terminal faced an attempted drone strike on Wednesday. Saudi officials stated that energy supplies remain undisrupted and the facility sustained no damage.

articles.headline: Gold prices surge 2% on safe-haven demand amid Mideast war
articles.summary: Spot gold climbed to $5,175.39 per ounce as escalating tensions drove investors toward the metal. Market uncertainty regarding the US-Iran conflict has significantly reversed recent gold price declines.

articles.headline: SpaceX adds Citigroup to lead IPO bank lineup
articles.summary: Elon Musk’s SpaceX has reportedly added Citigroup to the group of banks managing its upcoming initial public offering. The company is seeking a valuation of more than $1.75 trillion and could raise up to $50 billion in the debut.

articles.headline: Demand for actuaries surges ahead of insurance industry regulatory shifts
articles.summary: Indian insurance companies are facing a shortage of actuaries as the industry prepares for IFRS implementation by FY27. The shift toward risk-based capital frameworks is further driving the need for qualified professionals.

articles.headline: Apple achieves three trillion rupee revenue in India within five years
articles.summary: Driven by iPhone manufacturing through vendors like Foxconn and Tata, Apple's India revenue now rivals the country's top listed manufacturers. The company exported $23 billion worth of iPhones in 2025, making smartphones India’s largest export.

articles.headline: Petronet LNG invokes force majeure as Qatar energy production stops
articles.summary: India's largest LNG importer has notified offtakers of a force majeure after QatarEnergy halted production due to West Asia conflicts. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has disrupted energy flows, raising concerns over global gas supplies.

articles.headline: Geopolitical tensions disrupt destination wedding season for Indian couples in Dubai
articles.summary: Escalating conflict between Iran and Israel has led to the cancellation or postponement of numerous Indian weddings planned in the Gulf. Families are facing financial losses and travel uncertainty as airspace restrictions remain in place.

articles.headline: Aurobindo Pharma subsidiary launches generic oncology drug in US market
articles.summary: Eugia Pharma Specialities has introduced Pomalidomide capsules, a generic equivalent of Bristol Myers Squibb's Pomalyst, in the United States. The product targets a US market estimated at approximately $3.3 billion annually.

articles.headline: Godrej Properties acquires land in Gurugram and Kolkata for residential projects
articles.summary: Godrej Properties has secured land parcels in Gurugram and Kolkata with a total estimated revenue potential of ₹6,150 crore. The developments will focus on premium residential units and luxury lifestyle amenities.

articles.headline: Cognizant BPO segment grows faster than core business due to AI
articles.summary: Cognizant's CFO stated that generative AI is enhancing productivity and expanding the addressable market for business process outsourcing. The segment reported 9 per cent revenue growth in the fourth quarter, outperforming traditional services.

articles.headline: Walmart backed PhonePe plans IPO at valuation up to ten billion dollars
articles.summary: Indian fintech firm PhonePe is preparing for a listing that could raise over $900 million at a valuation between $9 billion and $10.5 billion. Major investors Walmart, Tiger Global, and Microsoft plan to trim or exit their holdings.

articles.headline: Fashion quick commerce startups shift focus from delivery speed to experience
articles.summary: Startups like ZILO and Libas are emphasizing personalized shopping and at-home trials over traditional 10-minute delivery metrics. The strategy aims to reduce high return rates and solve sizing uncertainties in the online apparel market.

articles.headline: MRF to invest 5,300 crore rupees in new Tamil Nadu plant
articles.summary: MRF has signed a memorandum of understanding with the Tamil Nadu government to build a greenfield tyre manufacturing facility in Sivaganga. The project is expected to create 1,000 direct jobs over a 12-year implementation period.

articles.headline: Global investors shift to long term platform deals in Indian real estate
articles.summary: Strategic partnerships between global institutional investors and Indian developers are replacing one-off asset acquisitions. The model focuses on scalable portfolios in office, logistics, and premium residential sectors with improved governance.

articles.headline: Mahindra denies receiving official notice of vehicle import suspension in Indonesia
articles.summary: Mahindra & Mahindra clarified that it has received no formal communication regarding a hold on vehicle imports from Indonesian authorities. The statement responds to reports suggesting a halt on orders for 35,000 Scorpio Pik Up units.

articles.headline: State Bank of India hires nearly six thousand junior associates in FY26
articles.summary: SBI has completed the recruitment of 5,783 junior associates following a selection process that drew over nine lakh applications. The bank has inducted over 18,000 employees across various roles during the current financial year.

articles.headline: Indian solar manufacturers seek new markets following US countervailing duties
articles.summary: US-imposed duties of 126 per cent have made the American market unviable for many Indian solar module exporters. Manufacturers are now looking to diversify export destinations as domestic production capacity reaches 144 gigawatts.

articles.headline: Axis Bank becomes Tesla's preferred financing partner for Indian market
articles.summary: Axis Bank has partnered with Tesla to offer tailored digital financing solutions and 10-year loans for premium electric vehicles. The collaboration aims to simplify the purchase journey for Tesla's diverse customer base in India.

articles.headline: DEG and Citi extend seventy six million dollar facility to Shriram Finance
articles.summary: A joint co-financing facility will support Shriram Finance in lending to MSMEs, smallholder farmers, and electric vehicle buyers. The funding is intended to accelerate financial inclusion and rural development in underserved segments.

articles.headline: Hundreds of tankers remain stranded as Strait of Hormuz remains closed
articles.summary: Over 200 ships, including oil and LNG tankers, are anchored off the Gulf coast as the vital shipping corridor remains paralyzed. President Trump has pledged navy escorts to resume energy exports amid soaring global prices.

articles.headline: Micron semiconductor plant inauguration marks significant step for India chip mission
articles.summary: The inauguration of Micron's $2.75 billion assembly and test facility in Gujarat represents a tangible advance for India's technology supply chain. While a major milestone, India still relies on imports for high-end 3-5 nm chips.

articles.headline: Indian sugar stocks rally as rising crude prices boost ethanol prospects
articles.summary: Shares of sugar companies rose sharply on expectations of higher ethanol prices and increased mandates. Rising crude oil costs due to the West Asia crisis are driving interest in biofuels as a competitive alternative.

articles.headline: PNGS Reva Diamond Jewellery shares list at seven per cent premium
articles.summary: PNGS Reva Diamond Jewellery made its market debut on Wednesday, closing at ₹414 against an issue price of ₹386. The company’s ₹380-crore IPO was subscribed seven times by investors.

articles.headline: Sedemac Mechatronics IPO subscribed twenty seven per cent on opening day
articles.summary: The initial public offering of Sedemac Mechatronics saw 27 per cent subscription on its first day of bidding. The company, valued at ₹5,971 crore, provides technology solutions for automotive and industrial manufacturers.

articles.headline: City gas distribution companies see stock declines amid LNG supply disruptions
articles.summary: Shares of Mahanagar Gas and Indraprastha Gas fell up to 10 per cent following reports of halted LNG production in Qatar. Analysts expect higher input costs and potential supply cuts to pressure margins for gas distributors.

articles.headline: Samsung exits telecom PLI scheme due to limited local demand
articles.summary: Samsung has withdrawn from the ₹12,195 crore production-linked incentive scheme for telecom gear after failing to meet investment targets. Only 15% of the total scheme outlay has been disbursed as it nears its conclusion.

articles.headline: Accenture wins $100 million Estée Lauder contract previously held by Wipro
articles.summary: Wipro faces a significant annual revenue dent as American cosmetics brand Estée Lauder shifts its IT functions to Accenture. The transition involves database management and AI deployment services.

articles.headline: Sebi caps portfolio overlap to encourage innovation in passive mutual funds
articles.summary: New market regulator rules mandate meaningful differentiation between thematic and sectoral fund schemes. The move is expected to catalyze growth in low-cost index funds and ETFs.

articles.headline: Fire at JSW Infrastructure oil terminal in Fujairah following drone intercept
articles.summary: Debris from an intercepted Iranian drone damaged one of 15 storage tanks at the UAE facility. No injuries were reported, and the company has secured the remaining tanks and insured the terminal.

articles.headline: Air cargo agents seek demurrage waivers amid West Asia flight disruptions
articles.summary: Widespread airspace closures have stranded export shipments at Indian airports, leading to rising penalty charges. Cargo agents have urged the government to intervene as rerouting increases freight costs.

articles.headline: Indian EPC firms face project risks in Gulf amid rising volatility
articles.summary: Shares of companies like Larsen & Toubro and KEC International fell as regional conflict threatens project execution. Sustained instability could delay tender closures and stretch receivable cycles.

articles.headline: Uber granted five-year transport aggregator licence renewal in Karnataka
articles.summary: The state authority renewal covers car and autorickshaw services through December 2026. Uber remains the only licensed player currently authorized for these categories in the state.

articles.headline: Capital Group and KKR launch private equity fund for retail investors
articles.summary: The new US Equity-plus fund combines private company investments with public stocks for everyday investors. The hybrid vehicle aims to offer retail participants access to institutional-grade strategies.

articles.headline: Samsung monitors global memory chip price surge to manage supply
articles.summary: The tech giant is tracking rising memory prices caused by market dynamics and increased AI demand. The company intends to curb impacts through strategic partnerships and local monitoring.

articles.headline: Pinterest authorizes $3.5 billion share buyback with Elliott Investment support
articles.summary: Activist investor Elliott is contributing $1 billion to help fund the social media company's repurchase program. Pinterest is also using $500 million of its own cash for the initiative.

articles.headline: Oister Global to launch ₹500 crore fund for startup secondary deals
articles.summary: The firm aims to provide liquidity to early investors in startups like BlackBuck and OfBusiness. Secondary deal volume in India rose 32% to ₹37,700 crore in the last fiscal year.

articles.headline: Lack of analyst coverage leaves Chinese AI listings flying blind
articles.summary: Nearly 80% of recent AI-related Hong Kong IPOs have fewer than three analysts covering them. Brokerages are struggling to keep pace with the rapid rate of new tech debuts.

articles.headline: Tata Group chair hopes West Asia conflict spares global supply chains
articles.summary: N. Chandrasekaran emphasized employee safety and mitigation plans for the conglomerate's regional operations. Tata Steel is already diversifying sources for critical materials like limestone.

articles.headline: Ship insurance premiums set to rise as war risks intensify
articles.summary: Freight carriers must pay additional war cover premiums for cargo passing through the Red Sea and Gulf. Insurers are issuing notices of cancellation to adjust pricing for the heightened risk levels.

articles.headline: Britannia Industries receives ₹6.37 crore GST demand notice
articles.summary: Authorities allege tax non-payment due to the incorrect classification of goods supplied over a four-year period. The company is currently exploring legal remedies and says there is no material financial impact.

articles.headline: AIF reforms offer accredited investors greater flexibility and private market access
articles.summary: New Sebi rules allow for separate co-investment vehicles and longer fund tenures of up to 10 years. While expanding opportunities, the changes increase concentration risks and liquidity trade-offs for retail participants.

articles.headline: Palantir stock surges as defense contracts bring bullish sentiment back
articles.summary: The software firm's shares jumped 13% over four sessions as US strikes on Iran highlighted its role in military data analysis. Palantir recently received a $10 billion contract from the US Army.

articles.headline: Small businesses drive India's e-commerce expansion through D2C channels
articles.summary: MSMEs are increasingly using WhatsApp and social media to bypass traditional marketplaces. The direct-to-consumer market is projected to reach $60 billion in sales by 2030.

articles.headline: Fintech Startup Moneyview Files Draft Papers for Public Listing
articles.summary: Accel-backed Moneyview has filed documents for an IPO aiming to raise 1,500 crore in fresh issue. The company plans to use the proceeds to capitalise its in-house NBFC and support loan disbursals.

articles.headline: Lufthansa CEO Spohr Uses Crisis Experience to Tackle Union Battles
articles.summary: Carsten Spohr is focusing on efficiency and management centralization to improve the airline's margins. Despite recent strikes and delivery delays, the company has seen improved punctuality and share price growth.

articles.headline: LNG Companies Tank 10% on Qatar Production Halt Concerns
articles.summary: Shares of Petronet LNG and Mahanagar Gas plunged as Iranian attacks disrupted Qatari gas production. Approximately 55% of India's LNG imports are impacted by the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: Life Insurers Focus on Annuity and Pension Products for Growth
articles.summary: Companies like SBI Life and HDFC Life are launching deferred and variable annuity products to target an aging population. The retirement segment currently accounts for nearly 19% of the total life insurance industry in India.

articles.headline: India Aims for 6% Share of Global Chemical Sector by 2030
articles.summary: The government is investing 13,000 crore into bio-pharma and three dedicated chemical parks to boost global trade presence. The goal is to reach a $1 trillion turnover by 2040 through enhanced infrastructure and research.

articles.headline: Hospitality Revenue Expected to Grow 12% in FY26 Says Icra
articles.summary: The Indian hotel industry is projected to maintain strong occupancy levels of 74% driven by corporate travel and weddings. Average room rates are also expected to rise as demand continues to outpace new supply.

articles.headline: SBI Recruits 5,783 Junior Associates to Strengthen Branch Operations
articles.summary: State Bank of India has added over 18,000 employees this fiscal year to enhance frontline capabilities and customer experience. The bank plans to onboard 16,000 employees annually to power its goal of doubling total business.

articles.headline: Adidas Forecasts Higher Profits Driven by Retro Sneaker Demand
articles.summary: The German sportswear brand expects an operating profit of 2.3 billion euros this year despite unfavorable currency moves. CEO Bjorn Gulden plans to expand dividends and share buybacks as the company targets mid-term growth.

articles.headline: Apparel Exporters Brace for Delays and Higher Shipping Costs to UAE
articles.summary: Indian textile firms are facing thin margins as freight and insurance costs surge due to sea route disruptions. The Middle East accounts for $1.8 billion in annual Indian apparel exports, which are now under execution pressure.

articles.headline: Zuckerberg Purchases $170-million Mansion on Miami's Billionaire Bunker Island
articles.summary: Meta founder Mark Zuckerberg has set a Miami-Dade County record with the purchase of a private island estate. He joins other Silicon Valley billionaires relocating to Florida amid talk of new taxes in California.

articles.headline: Oman Air restores services to India amid West Asia conflict
articles.summary: The airline has resumed flights to 10 Indian cities to assist expatriates stranded by regional airspace closures. Hundreds of travelers are currently using road routes from the UAE to Muscat to catch home-bound flights.

articles.headline: Morbi ceramic units face shutdown over propane supply crisis
articles.summary: Approximately 300 ceramic manufacturing units in Gujarat are set to halt operations as propane supplies dry up. The crisis stems from the U.S.-Iran conflict, which has disrupted fuel allocations to the industry.

articles.headline: PCB offers former captain Sarfaraz Ahmed Test head coach role
articles.summary: The Pakistan Cricket Board has invited 38-year-old Sarfaraz Ahmed to lead the Test side, potentially making him the youngest in the position. The team is currently preparing for the ICC World Test Championship.

articles.headline: IPL 2026 to begin in Bengaluru on March 28
articles.summary: Defending champions RCB will play the opening match at the Chinnaswamy Stadium. The full schedule will be released in two phases, pending the announcement of state election dates.

articles.headline: India women lose 2-1 to Vietnam in AFC Asian Cup opener
articles.summary: Despite an equalizer from debutant Sanfida Nongrum, India conceded a late goal in extra time to lose their group match. Vietnam's Ngan Thi Van Su scored twice to secure the win for the favorites.

articles.headline: Satwik-Chirag pair suffers early exit at All England championships
articles.summary: The top Indian doubles duo lost to Malaysia's Aaron Tai and Khang Khai Xing in the first round. The Malaysian teen pair produced a series of dominant smashes to overwhelm the former world number ones.

articles.headline: ICC reports record 500 million viewers for T20 World Cup
articles.summary: ICC chairman Jay Shah announced that the ongoing tournament has broken all broadcasting records in India. Digital viewership on JioHotstar also hit a peak of 60.5 million concurrent viewers.

articles.headline: Zimbabwe cricket team departs India following T20 World Cup exit
articles.summary: Zimbabwe Cricket confirmed the senior men's team is en route home via alternative travel arrangements after transit disruptions. The squad will return to Harare in batches starting Wednesday.

articles.headline: Satwik-Chirag suffer shock first round exit at All England Open
articles.summary: The world No. 4 Indian pair was defeated by Malaysian teenagers Kang Khai Xing and Aaron Tai in straight sets. The loss follows a trend of early exits for Indian badminton stars at the tournament.

articles.headline: Vietnam denies India football point with stoppage-time winning goal
articles.summary: The Indian women's team lost 1-2 to Vietnam in the AFC Asian Cup qualifiers after a 94th-minute strike. Sanfida Nongrum had scored the equalizer for India before the heart-breaking finish in Perth.

articles.headline: India and England prepare for T20 World Cup semi-final at Wankhede
articles.summary: The two giants face off in Mumbai with a spot in the final at stake. Both teams are looking to find their 'perfect game' after inconsistent performances earlier in the tournament.

articles.headline: Marco Jansen's fifty anchors South Africa in T20 World Cup semifinal
articles.summary: Marco Jansen scored 54 not out to lead South Africa to a total of 169 for 8 against New Zealand in the T20 World Cup semifinal. Despite a strong performance by New Zealand spinners, Jansen's rearguard attack provided a fighting total.

articles.headline: New Zealand reaches T20 World Cup final after Proteas rout
articles.summary: Finn Allen's unbeaten 100 led New Zealand to a nine-wicket victory over South Africa at Eden Gardens. The Black Caps chased down 170 with 43 balls to spare to secure their spot in the summit clash.

articles.headline: Vietnam defeats India in Women’s Asia Cup group fixture
articles.summary: Ngan Thi Van Su scored a brace, including a stoppage-time winner, to give Vietnam a 2-1 victory over India. Sanfida Nongrum scored the lone goal for the Blue Tigresses in the closely contested match in Perth.

articles.headline: Aravindh Chithambaram beats world champion Gukesh in Prague
articles.summary: Defending champion Aravindh defeated compatriot D. Gukesh in the sixth round of the Prague International Chess Festival. The loss relegates the reigning world champion to the bottom of the Masters section rankings.

articles.headline: Pakistan drops Babar Azam for Bangladesh ODI series
articles.summary: Selectors have excluded Babar Azam and Saim Ayub from the 15-member squad following poor T20 World Cup performances. Six uncapped players have been included for the three-match series starting March 11.

articles.headline: Satwik-Chirag pair crashes out of All England Open
articles.summary: World No. 4 pair Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty lost in the first round to Malaysia's Aaron Tai and Khai Xing Kang. The Indians failed to capitalize on game points in the first set, eventually losing 21-23, 12-21.

articles.headline: Formula One technical reset faces first test in Melbourne
articles.summary: The new F1 era begins at the Australian Grand Prix with major changes to chassis and power unit regulations. Teams are concerned about energy deployment and the unknown impact of 100% sustainable fuels during racing.

articles.headline: Former Hindustan Times editor-in-chief HK Dua passes away at 88
articles.summary: Veteran journalist H K Dua, who headed four major national newspapers and advised two Prime Ministers, died on Wednesday. He also served as India's ambassador to Denmark and was a Rajya Sabha member.

articles.headline: Pokémon franchise marks 30 years as highest-grossing global media property
articles.summary: The Japanese brand has reached $150 billion in total revenue since its 1996 launch. It has sold nearly 500 million video games and 75 billion trading cards worldwide.

articles.headline: Actor Allu Arjun initiates legal action over viral podcast claims
articles.summary: The row highlights the reputational risks celebrities face from unverified claims and algorithmic amplification. The brand strategist involved has since retracted her statements and issued an apology.

articles.headline: Telangana High Court upholds gift deed despite grandfather's neglect claims
articles.summary: The court set aside an order cancelling a house gift deed executed by a 90-year-old man in favor of his grandson. Judges ruled that the senior citizens' welfare authorities lacked the jurisdiction to annul the registered document.

articles.headline: Former software engineer dies by suicide over alleged dowry harassment
articles.summary: Bengaluru police arrested the husband of a 35-year-old woman after her father filed a complaint citing persistent dowry demands. The family claims they had already provided Rs 10 lakh and gold at the time of marriage.

articles.headline: Apparel store manager attacked with machete over Instagram exchange
articles.summary: A 42-year-old manager in Basaveshwaranagar was assaulted after a misunderstanding regarding a former colleague's Instagram account. The attacker, the colleague's husband, threatened further violence before fleeing the scene.

articles.headline: Nephew kills polio survivor uncle and attempts to fake accidental fall
articles.summary: A 23-year-old man was arrested in Austin Town for murdering his uncle following a property dispute. Medical reports confirming multiple broken ribs exposed the suspect's original claim that the victim fell while intoxicated.

articles.headline: High Court rules right to know arrest grounds includes foreigners
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court observed that constitutional safeguards under Article 22 are person-centric and extend to foreign nationals. The court granted relief to two Nigerians while directing their deportation due to overstaying.

articles.headline: Businessman cheated of Rs 25 lakh in KIMS medical seat scam
articles.summary: Three individuals were booked for allegedly forging government receipts and promising a fake MBBS seat at a Bengaluru institute. The victim discovered the fraud after the accused failed to secure the seat or return funds.

articles.headline: Rajasthan to table 'disturbed areas' property sales regulation bill
articles.summary: The proposed legislation will allow the state to designate localities as disturbed areas, requiring prior permission for property transfers. The bill aims to protect tenants and regulate sales in areas prone to mob violence.

articles.headline: Court grants bail to father in fatal Ghatkopar SUV crash
articles.summary: A sessions court granted bail to Valaji Raja Bhushan, the father of a minor accused of killing a businessman in February. The judge noted the father was not present in the vehicle during the accident.

articles.headline: SIMI member convicted for terror ties despite 7/11 blast acquittal
articles.summary: Ehtesham Siddiqui was sentenced to seven years in jail for membership in the banned Students Islamic Movement of India. This follows his recent acquittal in the 2006 Mumbai train blasts case.

articles.headline: Mehul Choksi extradition hearing concludes in Antwerp; judgment pending
articles.summary: A Belgian court heard arguments regarding fugutive diamantaire Mehul Choksi's extradition to India. Choksi's lawyers alleged he was kidnapped and tortured by Indian authorities in 2021 to prevent his return.

articles.headline: NCLT initiates insolvency proceedings against Marvel Group promoter Vishwajeet Jhawar
articles.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal has ordered insolvency proceedings against Vishwajeet Jhawar over an outstanding debt of ₹226.89 crore. The petition was filed by APRN Enterprises following a failure to repay financial facilities granted in 2017.

articles.headline: Nexus Select Mall Management settles REIT regulation lapse case with Sebi
articles.summary: Nexus Select Mall Management paid ₹24.4 lakh to settle allegations of violating REIT regulations regarding net worth maintenance. The proceedings concerned the firm's failure to inform Sebi about material changes in its financials.

articles.headline: Sebi issues new framework for non bank custodians to offer services
articles.summary: The regulator has allowed custodians to provide unregulated financial services through separate strategic business units. Custodians must maintain separate books and ensure net worth requirements are met excluding these units.

articles.headline: ED questions Sabarimala Tantri in gold theft money laundering case
articles.summary: The Directorate of Enforcement questioned chief priest Kandararu Rajeevaru for four hours regarding his association with prime accused Unnikrishnan Potti. The probe focuses on financial transactions related to a reported gold theft.

articles.headline: Domestic help flees with gold and cash worth Rs 23 lakh
articles.summary: A recently hired cook allegedly stole diamond jewelry and Rs 3 lakh from a businessman's residence in Jakkasandra. Police have registered a case and are searching for the suspect, who is a native of Bihar.

articles.headline: Five members of Bengaluru family killed in Andhra road crash
articles.summary: A family traveling to Tirumala died after their car crashed into the rear of a moving truck at high speed. Preliminary investigations suggest the car was traveling at 130 kmph at the time of impact.

articles.headline: Cab driver arrested for burglary after overhearing family's travel plans
articles.summary: An aggregator cab driver allegedly broke into a businessman's house in Bengaluru after dropping the family at the railway station. Police recovered 180 grams of stolen gold from locations in Karnataka and Tamil Nadu.

articles.headline: Rohit Pawar claims Baramati plane crash report contains major errors
articles.summary: The NCP (SP) MLA flagged inconsistencies between CCTV footage and the AAIB's preliminary report on the fatal January crash. He questioned claims regarding aircraft banking direction and the condition of the black boxes.

articles.headline: Biker decapitated in high-speed hit-and-run on Palm Beach Road
articles.summary: A 23-year-old motorcyclist died on the spot and his teenage pillion rider was critically injured in Navi Mumbai. Police are scanning CCTV footage to identify the vehicle involved in the early morning crash.

articles.headline: Anand Patwardhan barred from meeting protestor at Mumbai University
articles.summary: Documentary filmmaker Anand Patwardhan was stopped at the Kalina campus gate while attempting to support a peaceful protestor. The university claimed it had no prior information regarding his visit.

articles.headline: Housekeeping worker stabbed to death following altercation in Navi Mumbai
articles.summary: Rahul Jayvinder Bumbak was killed in Kamothe after being confronted by two 19-year-olds for allegedly staring at them. Both suspects have been arrested and the murder weapon has been seized.

articles.headline: Thane man arrested for raping minor and extorting 50,000 rupees
articles.summary: Prathamesh Desai was apprehended for sexually assaulting a 15-year-old schoolgirl and blackmailing her for money over six months. The victim confided in her parents after being overwhelmed by the abuse.

articles.headline: Film director booked for cheating actresses of 47 lakh rupees
articles.summary: Avinash Jadhav allegedly duped Varsha Usgaonkar and Mrunalini Jambhale by promising high returns on real estate investments. Police registered the case after Jadhav failed to repay the invested amounts.

articles.headline: Veteran journalist and former Rajya Sabha member H K Dua dies at 88
articles.summary: Eminent journalist H K Dua, who edited three of India's leading newspapers and served as an ambassador, passed away on Wednesday. He was also a nominated member of the Rajya Sabha and was known for political insight.

articles.headline: Indian government monitors security situation for seafarers in the Persian Gulf
articles.summary: Shipping Minister Sarbananda Sonowal announced that monitoring mechanisms have been activated to protect Indian maritime assets and personnel. The government remains in touch with international agencies to ensure the welfare of sailors.

articles.headline: Defence Ministry recommends procurement of sixty indigenous Ghatak combat drones
articles.summary: The Defence Procurement Board has recommended moving forward with the proposal to acquire 60 Ghatak unmanned combat aerial vehicles. Developed by DRDO, the stealth flying-wing drone is designed for deep-strike missions in defended airspace.

articles.headline: India and Canada sign five-year MoU to boost agri-food cooperation
articles.summary: The agreement between NIFTEM-K and USask focuses on joint research and faculty exchanges in food technology. It aims to advance sustainable food systems and value-chain development.

articles.headline: India Supports Myanmar-led Peace Process Says Jaishankar
articles.summary: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar stated that India backs a peace process owned and led by Myanmar to ensure lasting stability. He highlighted Myanmar's role at the confluence of India's 'Neighbourhood First' and 'Act East' policies.

articles.headline: Strict Restrictions Remain in Kashmir Following Protests Over Khamenei Killing
articles.summary: Authorities have imposed curbs and suspended mobile internet in parts of Kashmir after massive protests against the death of the Iranian leader. Schools remain shut and police have filed cases against local political leaders for misleading content.

articles.headline: Finnish President Alexander Stubb Begins Four-Day Visit to India
articles.summary: President Stubb arrived in New Delhi to strengthen bilateral ties in trade, investment, and technology. He will meet Prime Minister Modi and deliver a keynote address at the Raisina Dialogue.

articles.headline: Five Bengaluru residents killed in Chittoor road accident
articles.summary: Five people traveling from Bengaluru to Tirumala were killed when their car rammed a stationary lorry on the highway. The deceased include three women and two men aged between 33 and 71.

articles.headline: Army foils infiltration attempt along LoC in J&K’s Rajouri
articles.summary: Troops opened fire on suspected infiltrators in the Turkandi area early Wednesday morning, prompting a massive search operation. Heightened alert remains in force across the sector to prevent terrorist movement.

articles.headline: Parents urge Karnataka government to regulate arbitrary school fee hikes
articles.summary: Parent groups are demanding a structured law to control annual tuition fee increases of 15% by private schools. They propose a regulatory framework similar to Delhi’s model, involving parent committees in the decision-making process.

articles.headline: VTU seeks state permission to establish engineering campuses abroad
articles.summary: Visvesvaraya Technological University is looking to expand into countries like Saudi Arabia and South Africa following international requests. The move requires an amendment to the Act governing the university's state-only jurisdiction.

articles.headline: Maharashtra tables bill to legalize Maratha quota in private colleges
articles.summary: The state government introduced amendments to the 2015 Act to clarify Maratha reservation in professional educational institutions. The bill also establishes an appellate authority for fee hike and admission disputes.

articles.headline: US business schools seek Indian university tie ups as student numbers drop
articles.summary: Facing a 45 per cent decline in Indian student enrolments, US institutions are pursuing credit-sharing and joint degree programs with local universities. Schools like Rutgers and Olin are looking for collaborations to overcome affordability and visa challenges.

articles.headline: KSPCB issues closure notices to 17 units over lead contamination
articles.summary: The Karnataka Pollution Control Board acted after joint committee findings detected lead levels beyond safety limits in vegetables. Violations include missing effluent treatment plants and inadequate chimney heights in industrial estates.

articles.headline: Nandi Hills wildfire brought under control by forest officials
articles.summary: A fire that broke out at the hillock late Tuesday was contained by Wednesday morning without significant damage to flora. Authorities are investigating if the blaze was an accidental wildfire or a deliberate act.

articles.headline: Mumbai records 38.7 degrees as early heatwave triggers yellow alert
articles.summary: The city experienced its earliest March temperature spike in 10 years at the Santacruz weather station. The IMD attributed the rise to easterly winds and anticyclonic circulation over the region.

articles.headline: Global plastic treaty talks in Japan described as constructive
articles.summary: Informal discussions in Tokyo aimed to restart negotiations for a landmark pollution treaty. While no official deal was reached, delegates reported progress ahead of a May meeting.

articles.headline: TERI and Global Carbon Council to Set Up India's First Digital Carbon Marketplace
articles.summary: A new partnership aims to unlock carbon finance for community-scale renewable energy projects in rural India. The initiative will allow small household projects to participate in international carbon markets through tradable credits.

articles.headline: Kochi launches Kerala’s first municipal compressed biogas plant
articles.summary: A new facility at Brahmapuram converts 150 tonnes of organic waste daily into 5.6 tonnes of purified biogas and organic manure. The BPCL-funded project aims to solve the city's waste crisis following the 2023 dump yard fire.

articles.headline: Greenland’s fishermen face economic uncertainty from warming Arctic
articles.summary: Rapid climate change is disrupting traditional ice fishing and forcing shifts in Greenland’s seafood-driven economy. Warming waters are driving fish deeper, making catches less predictable for local communities.

articles.headline: IISc launches 'Moonshot' project for advanced brain co-processors
articles.summary: The Indian Institute of Science is developing neuromorphic hardware to restore brain function for stroke survivors. The initiative is funded by the Pratiksha Trust and involves both implantable and non-invasive technologies.

articles.headline: Government expects sharp rise in green hydrogen production capacity by 2029
articles.summary: India targets a production capacity of 200,000 tonnes of green hydrogen over the next three years as projects in steel and transport commissioned. The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy is working on standards and subsidy schemes for smaller plants.

articles.headline: Standard air conditioner uses daily energy of average Indian in 44 minutes
articles.summary: Data reveals that running a 1-kW AC unit for under an hour consumes 0.734 kWh of electricity. This amount equals the total average daily per capita energy consumption in India.

articles.headline: India transitions to low-fertility society as birth rates decline
articles.summary: Data from NFHS-5 shows that most Indian states now record fertility rates below the replacement level of 2.1 children per woman. Rising women's education and improved child survival rates are cited as primary drivers of this demographic shift.

articles.headline: Urban traffic noise linked to chronic health risks in India
articles.summary: Average urban noise levels reaching 100 dB are causing measurable physiological damage beyond hearing loss. Studies show chronic noise activates stress hormones that regulate immunity and cardiovascular health.

articles.headline: Indian IT firms pivot to AI-led solutions for revenue growth
articles.summary: India's top IT firms are shifting toward automated, AI-enabled delivery models to improve efficiency and revenue per employee. Experts project AI-led revenues could reach $10-12 billion as companies expand partnerships with AI-native firms.

articles.headline: GX Group and Qualcomm partner for AI enabled Wi-Fi routers
articles.summary: European firm GX Group has collaborated with Qualcomm to manufacture AI-powered Wi-Fi 7 gateway solutions in India. The platform was launched by the Union Telecom Minister at the Mobile World Congress.

articles.headline: Reliance Jio integrates AI to transition into a token services provider
