articles.summary: Jio Platforms CEO Mathew Oommen announced plans to transform the telecom provider into a scalable AI-driven token service. The shift aims to leverage connectivity infrastructure to build foundational layers for the digital economy.

articles.headline: Apple introduces five hundred dollar MacBook Neo to target budget market
articles.summary: Apple has launched its most affordable laptop yet, featuring a 13-inch screen and an iPhone A18 Pro chip. The $599 MacBook Neo marks a strategic shift for the company into the low-end Windows PC and Chromebook market.

articles.headline: Google releases March Pixel Drop with enhanced AI and search features
articles.summary: The latest update for Pixel devices introduces new capabilities for the Gemini assistant and multi-object recognition for Circle to Search. Other features include a unified desktop mode and updated themes rolling out to users.

articles.headline: VSCO introduces new Galleries app for contributor led image collections
articles.summary: Visual Supply Co has launched VSCO Galleries, a standalone application allowing users to curate shared image collections. The app is currently available for iPhone users, with a desktop version planned for future release.

articles.headline: India notifies first national standards for cloud computing and ethical AI
articles.summary: The Bureau of Indian Standards has formalized definitions and performance metrics aligned with international ISO-IEC frameworks. The voluntary standards provide a technical backbone for India's AI and data centre expansion.

articles.headline: Infosys and Intel expand collaboration for enterprise AI production
articles.summary: The partnership integrates Infosys Topaz Fabric with Intel's high-performance processors and AI accelerators. The tie-up is designed to help global firms transition AI pilots into full-scale production.

articles.headline: Jumbotail and NEC partner to digitalize Indian kirana stores
articles.summary: Japan's NEC Corp will make a strategic investment in Jumbotail to provide enterprise tools to grocery retailers. The partnership aims to modernize traditional retail through digital inventory and procurement solutions.

articles.headline: Meta tests AI shopping chatbot to compete with ChatGPT and Gemini
articles.summary: The new research feature provides tailored product suggestions based on user interests and location. The bot generates carousels of images with merchant links but lacks direct payment options.

articles.headline: Pentagon rifts with Anthropic over AI safeguards and military control
articles.summary: The Trump administration has designated AI firm Anthropic a supply-chain risk after a breakdown in trust regarding autonomous weapons. The dispute involves clashing ideologies between CEO Dario Amodei and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.

articles.headline: Hyundai unveils Atlas humanoid robot to compete with Tesla Optimus
articles.summary: The company plans to deploy the robots on assembly lines by 2028 to reduce labor costs and improve safety. Hyundai is investing $6.3 billion in a new robot factory and AI data centre in South Korea.

articles.headline: Apple launches M5-chip MacBooks with increased base storage
articles.summary: Updated MacBook Air and Pro models feature significant gains in on-device AI capabilities. Apple effectively lowered prices by doubling standard storage tiers across the new lineup.

articles.headline: Anthropic accuses Chinese labs of illicitly extracting AI model capabilities
articles.summary: The company claims DeepSeek and others used 'industrial-scale campaigns' to distil Claude's features. The dispute highlights the difficulty of protecting intellectual property in the global AI race.

articles.headline: Frontier AI models democratize coding capabilities for non-technical users
articles.summary: Advances in AI allow individuals to translate plain-English descriptions into functional software and mobile apps. This shift is predicted to blur traditional boundaries between developers and service providers.

articles.headline: Vietnam becomes first SE Asian nation to regulate artificial intelligence
articles.summary: A new law requires human oversight of generative AI and clear labeling of deepfakes. The framework applies to both domestic developers and global technology companies operating in the country.

articles.headline: MWC Barcelona focuses on sovereign AI and soaring hardware costs
articles.summary: Phone manufacturers are integrating companion AI while facing rising RAM prices. Innovations showcased include smartphones designed for pet interaction and portable AI companions.

articles.headline: Ford Integrates AI While Keeping Humans in Control
articles.summary: Ford's AI chief Franziska Bell stated the automaker is using artificial intelligence to automate manual tasks across design and factory operations. The company emphasizes a 'human-AI team' strategy to augment ingenuity rather than replace workers.

articles.headline: Bitcoin Surpasses $71,000 as Crypto Markets Recover from Initial Shock
articles.summary: Digital assets rallied on Wednesday with Bitcoin hitting a one-month high despite broader market volatility. US-based Bitcoin ETFs saw over $680 million in inflows as investors viewed the dip as a buying opportunity.

articles.headline: Automakers Face Acute Shortage of Skilled Engineers for Clean Tech
articles.summary: India will require up to 200,000 engineers with expertise in EV and alternative fuel technologies this year. Major vehicle makers are struggling to find talent for hybrid and hydrogen powertrain research and development.

articles.headline: OpenAI Eyes NATO Contract to Deploy AI on Unclassified Networks
articles.summary: Sam Altman's company is seeking a deal to deploy its technology across NATO networks following a similar agreement with the Pentagon. The move comes as OpenAI looks to expand its presence in government and security sectors.

articles.headline: Brokerages Split on Future of Indian IT as AI Automates Tasks
articles.summary: Analysts are divided on whether AI will cause revenue deflation or expand the market for IT service providers. Some firms have downgraded major IT stocks, predicting a 10% hit to earnings as automation replaces routine managed services.

articles.headline: Apple Raises Prices for New M5-powered MacBook Pro and Air
articles.summary: Apple has updated its laptop lineup with faster M5 chips but increased retail prices due to an industrywide memory crunch. The company also revamped its external monitor models alongside the new computer releases.

articles.headline: U.S. Department of Defence labels Anthropic a supply chain risk
articles.summary: The Pentagon restricted use of the AI model Claude after Anthropic refused to allow its tools for domestic surveillance and autonomous weaponry. OpenAI subsequently reached an agreement with the DoD containing specific safety oversight protocols.

articles.headline: Study finds inconsistent website blocking practices among Indian ISPs
articles.summary: Research into Indian internet providers revealed that only a small fraction of blocked domains are universally restricted across all networks. The report highlights that ISPs use varying technical mechanisms due to a lack of specific legal mandates.

articles.headline: US submarine sinks Iranian warship in the Indian Ocean
articles.summary: A US submarine sank the Iranian frigate IRIS Dena off the southern coast of Sri Lanka, resulting in dozens of casualties. The vessel was reportedly returning to Iran after participating in naval exercises organized by India in the Bay of Bengal.

articles.headline: Mojtaba Khamenei emerges as favorite to succeed slain Iranian Supreme Leader
articles.summary: Sources indicate that Mojtaba Khamenei is favored to succeed his father as Iran’s top ruler following the latter's death in an airstrike. The powerful son maintains close ties with the Revolutionary Guards, who have taken a wartime leadership role.

articles.headline: Pakistan army launches air strike on Bagram airbase in Afghanistan
articles.summary: Pakistan carried out a major attack on the Bagram Air Base as part of Operation Ghazab lil Haq against the Afghan Taliban. The operation follows a series of terrorist attacks originating from Afghan soil targeting Pakistani border locations.

articles.headline: Indian Rupee Breaches 92 Mark Against Dollar for First Time
articles.summary: The rupee hit a record low of 92.15 against the US dollar as investors shifted to safe-haven assets. Central bank interventions were reportedly required to arrest further steep declines during the trading session.

articles.headline: Indian Rupee hits record low of 92.15 against dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee plummeted to a record low of 92.15 due to rising crude oil prices and sustained geopolitical tensions. The RBI is expected to maintain calibrated interventions to manage liquidity and moderate the pace of depreciation.

articles.headline: Nitish Kumar Moves to Rajya Sabha as BJP Prepares for CM
articles.summary: Bihar CM Nitish Kumar will file his nomination for the Rajya Sabha, marking the end of a long socialist leadership era in the state. Sources indicate the BJP will appoint the next Chief Minister, a first for the party in Bihar.

articles.headline: MVA Agrees to Back Sharad Pawar for Rajya Sabha Seat
articles.summary: The Maha Vikas Aghadi coalition has resolved internal wrangling to nominate NCP (SP) president Sharad Pawar for the Rajya Sabha. The decision comes just one day before the filing deadline, following meetings to address concerns from the UBT Sena.

articles.headline: Finnish President arrives in India to strengthen bilateral and technology ties
articles.summary: President Alexander Stubb has begun a four-day state visit to India to discuss cooperation in trade, investment, and critical technology. The visit includes high-level talks with Prime Minister Narendra Modi to firm up multiple agreements.

articles.headline: India supports Myanmar-led peace process for lasting development
articles.summary: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar emphasized India's commitment to a peace process owned by Myanmar during a building inauguration in Yangon. He noted that Myanmar is central to India's 'Neighbourhood First' and 'Act East' policies.

articles.headline: India ranks second globally in childhood obesity: World Atlas
articles.summary: A new report estimates nearly 41 million children in India are overweight or obese as of 2025. The World Obesity Federation warns that India is off track to meet global targets due to poor physical activity and diet.

articles.headline: PhonePe targets 10.5 billion dollar valuation for upcoming IPO
articles.summary: The Walmart-backed fintech firm aims to raise nearly $1 billion through an offer that will see major investors trim their stakes. The valuation is lower than earlier private funding rounds due to cooling fintech sentiment.

articles.headline: IIFL Finance Considers Exit from Microfinance Subsidiary Samasta
articles.summary: IIFL Finance is reportedly in preliminary talks with Piramal Finance and other suitors to sell its microfinance arm. The move is part of a long-term plan to bring new investors into the subsidiary following recent sector volatility.

articles.headline: Finn Allen blasts record century to lead New Zealand to final
articles.summary: New Zealand's Finn Allen hit the fastest century in T20 World Cup history as his side defeated South Africa by nine wickets. They will face either India or England in the final on March 8.

articles.headline: India meets England in T20 World Cup semifinal at Wankhede
articles.summary: The defending champions face England in their third consecutive T20 World Cup semifinal meeting. India seeks to maintain its balanced form against an England side led by Harry Brook and speedster Jofra Archer.

articles.headline: Oxford's Ashmolean Museum returns 16th-century bronze idol to India
articles.summary: The University of Oxford museum is returning a bronze statue of Saint Thirumangai Alvar traced back to a Tamil Nadu temple. The handover follows research confirming the idol was photographed in its original temple in 1957.

articles.headline: High Court quashes order allowing home-cooked food for Darshan
articles.summary: The court ruled that undertrial prisoners cannot be granted home food without prior medical advice and formal recommendations. Indiscriminate concessions would create chaos in prison management and nutritional standard compliance.

articles.headline: Bombay High Court rules divorce cannot be granted on WhatsApp alone
articles.summary: The court set aside a family court decree that used derogatory WhatsApp messages as the sole proof of mental cruelty. Judges ruled that such evidence must be formally proved through witness testimony and cross-examination.
